title: Infant Joy
content:
"I have no name;
    I am but two days old."
    What shall I call thee?
    "I happy am,
    Joy is my name."
    Sweet joy befall thee!

    Pretty joy!
    Sweet joy, but two days old.
    Sweet Joy I call thee:
    Thou dost smile,
    I sing the while;
    Sweet joy befall thee!
meaning:
William Blake's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Infant Joy"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
